questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In each of the questions, there are four sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are incorrect in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly:
I. The Prime Minister’s Economic Advisory Council had spelt out
II. a ‘clear road map’ for the government to ramp up job creation
III. and infrastructure financing avenues while investing
IV. more resources on health, education and skill development.
questions.solution:
Fragment IV: more resources on health, education and skill development.

Here, usage of the verb 'on' is erroneous here as the correct preposition that follows the verb 'invest' is 'in' and not 'on'.

Ex. He invested his money in stocks.

Correct fragment: more resources in health, education and skill development.

Option A is hence the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: The given sentence has been broken up into four different parts. The error, if any, will be in any one part of the sentence. Select the option which contains the part of the sentence which has an error (spelling, grammatical or contextual). If there is no error, choose option E.
Euthanasia is basically killing of (A) / patients by doctors at the instance (B) / of the patient in order to free him or her from (C) / exhilarating pain or from terminal illness. (D) / No Error (E)
options: patients by doctors at the instance
of the patient in order to free him or her from
exhilarating pain or from terminal illness.
questions.solution:
The fragment D of the sentence is erroneous.

Reason:

The usage of the word “exhilarating” is contextually erroneous. “Exhilarating” means “making one feel very happy, animated, or elated; thrilling”.

Pain can never be an ‘exhilarating’ thing.

To make the sentence correct, ‘exhilarating’ might be replaced with ‘excruciating’ which means ‘agonizing; severe’.

Correct Sentence:

Euthanasia is basically killing of patients by doctors at the instance of the patient in order to free him or her from excruciating pain or from terminal illness.

Hence, option D is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: The given sentence has been broken up into four different parts. The error, if any, will be in any one part of the sentence. Select the option which contains the part of the sentence which has an error (spelling, grammatical or contextual). If there is no error, choose option E.
The cancer patient is comparatively better (A) / today and the doctor hopes (B) / that he will go into (C) / remission soon. (D) / No Error (E)
options: The cancer patient is comparatively better
questions.solution:
The fragment A of the sentence is erroneous.

Reason:

“Better” is the comparative degree of ‘good’. “Better” is perfectly capable of denoting the sense of comparison in the given sentence.

Hence, the usage of the word ‘comparatively’ is superfluous and inappropriate in the given sentence.

Correct Sentence:

The cancer patient is better today and the doctor hopes that he will go into remission soon.

Hence, option A is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In each of the questions, there are four sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are incorrect in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly:
I. Memories of the killing of 
II. thousands people in the aftermath of
III. the 2007 elections should evens score the
IV. urgency of bringing the current fluid situation within control.
questions.solution:
Sentence II: thousands people in the aftermaths of

The sentence is incorrect because of use of ‘s’ in thousand.
 
‘s’ is used in thousand when it is used without any other plural subject.
 
Ex. Thousands were injured in the train accident yesterday.
 
But if thousand is used with another plural subject ,’s’ is omitted.
 
Ex. There are thousand ways to solve this problem.

Correct sentence- thousand people in the aftermaths of
 
Sentence III: the 2007 elections should evens score the
 
Use of the word “evens score” is inappropriate here, as it means to repay.

The correct word which goes with the context is ‘underscore’, which means to underline or emphasize.

Correct sentence- the 2007 elections should underscore the
 
Hence option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In each of the questions, there are four sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are incorrect in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly:
I. A comprehensive solution demands
II. that the government of Delhi, Punjab,
III. Haryana and Uttar Pradesh, assisted of the Centre,
IV. address farm residue burning from construction dust.
questions.solution:
Sentence II: that the government of Delhi, Punjab,

The sentence talks about the government of more than one city, thus “government” should be replaced with ‘governments’.

Correct sentence- that the governments of Delhi, Punjab,

Sentence III: Haryana and Uttar Pradesh, assisted of the Centre,

The sentence has an error with respect to use of wrong preposition.

Assistance means help which is always rendered by someone. Hence “of” should be replaced with preposition ‘by’.

Correct sentence- Haryana and Uttar Pradesh, assisted by the Centre,

Sentence IV: address farm residue burning from construction dust.

Use of preposition “from” in the sentence is illogical, because burning of farm residue and construction dust are two entirely different things.

For such purpose , a conjunction is used and not the preposition.

Hence “from” will be replaced by ‘and’.

Correct sentence- address farm residue burning and construction dust.

Hence option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In each of the questions, there are four sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are incorrect in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly:
I. If supreme court
II. decide not to uphold the latest victory
III. for the incumbent, the absence of a genuine contest
IV. could prove to be a critical factor.
questions.solution:
Sentence I: If supreme court

This is erroneous with respect to omission of article ‘the’ before supreme court.

Certain words like Supreme, latest etc. denotes the superlative degree of adjective and preceded by ‘the’.

Correct sentence- If the supreme court
 
Sentence II: decide not to uphold the latest victory

This sentence is also having an error with respect to wrong usage of verb.

As per the singular subject which is “supreme court” here, the verb should be singular here.

Correct sentence: decides not to uphold the latest victory

Hence option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Computers are capable of (A) / doing extreme complicated work (B) / in all branches (C) / of learning. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part B, as the word before 'complicated' is to add information to the adjective 'complicated', it has to be an adverb. 

The adjective 'extreme', hence, must be replaced with the adverb 'extremely' to make it a grammatically correct sentence.

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
There is a growing lack (A) / of sensitivity and (B) / respect towards our (C) / fellow creature. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part D, the noun 'creature' must be replaced with its plural form 'creatures' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
The doctor adviced Mr. Murugan that, (A) / because of his severe cramps, (B) / he should lay in the (C) / bed for a few days. (D) / No error (E)
options: The doctor adviced Mr. Murugan that,
questions.solution:
In part C, the verb 'lay' which means 'to put something somewhere in a gentle way' must be replaced with 'lie', which means 'to rest', to make the sentence grammatically correct. 

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
This happened (A) / just exactly (B) / five years ago (C) / when I was in Mumbai. (D)  No error (E)
questions.solution:
In some specific context, we use the adverb 'just' to emphasize that something is happening or has happened at exactly the moment that you are talking about.

In this sentence, use of the adverb 'exactly' beside 'just' is redundant and hence must be removed to make it a grammatically correct sentence.

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: The given sentence has been broken up into four different parts. The error, if any, will be in any one part of the sentence. Select the option which contains the part of the sentence which has an error (spelling, grammatical or contextual). If there is no error, choose option E.
Millions of people may be (A) / displaced and thousands of cities (B) / may drown because of melting glaciers (C) / and raising sea levels. (D) / No Error (E)
options: may drown because of melting glaciers
questions.solution:
The fragment D of the sentence is erroneous.

Reason:

The usage of the word ‘raising’ is erroneous and inappropriate.

“Raise” means ‘lift or move to a higher position or level / increase the amount, level, or strength of’.

A third-party-subject is necessary to perform the task of ‘raising (something)’. But ocean levels rise on its own. One might argue that ocean levels are rising due to human activity. But it’s too distant a relation. ‘Raise’ is used when ‘something’ is purposefully lifted. But it was not the intention of humans to raise ocean levels. It happened unintentionally. Therefore, the correct word in place of ‘raising’ would be ‘rising’.

Correct Sentence:

Millions of people may be displaced and thousands of cities may drown because of melting glaciers and rising sea levels.

Hence, option D is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In each of the questions, there are four sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are incorrect in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly:
I.The services sector offered some resemblance of stability, 
II. growing at 8.7% compared with 9% in the same quarter
III. last year, but a deeper look suggests this
IV. was drove by a rise in trade-related GVA to 11.1% (from 8.9%).
questions.solution:
Statement I: The services sector offered some resemblance of stability,

The word ‘resemblance’ means similarity which is not fitting in to the context, thus inappropriate here.

It should be replaced with ‘semblance’, which means appearance or aura”. This surely adds relevance to the context.

Correct sentence- The services sector offered some semblance of stability,

Statement III: last year, but a deeper look suggests this

Omission of ‘that’ after suggests caused error in this sentence.

Whenever a suggestion, advice or opinion is expressed it should be followed by that.
 
Correct sentence- last year, but a deeper look suggests that this
 
Statement IV: was drove by a rise in trade-related GVA to 11.1% (from 8.9%).
 
The sentence is expressed in passive voice, thus ‘was’ is followed by the third form of verb as per the grammar rules.

Third from of drive is “driven”, which should be used in place of ‘drove.’
 
Correct sentence- was driven by a rise in trade-related GVA to 11.1% (from 8.9%).
 
Hence option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: The given sentence has been broken up into four different parts. The error, if any, will be in any one part of the sentence. Select the option which contains the part of the sentence which has an error (spelling, grammatical or contextual). If there is no error, choose option E.
To an insurance company, (A) / the law relating to (B) / the principles of subrogation and (C) / contribution are of great importance. (D) / No Error (E)
questions.solution:
The fragment D of the sentence is erroneous.

Reason:

The subject of the sentence is ‘the law’, NOT ‘the principles of subrogation and contribution’. Hence, the usage of the verb ‘are’ in fragment D is erroneous. The correct verb in place of ‘are’ should have been ‘is’.

Correct Sentence:

To an insurance company, the law relating to the principles of subrogation and contribution is of great importance.

Hence, option D is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In each of the questions, there are four sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are incorrect in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly:
I. It is unconscionable for governments, through indifference and inaction,
II. to be subjected to citizens to such toxic air, and cause extreme suffering
III. especially among people with respiratory ailments
IV. and impaired lung function.
questions.solution:
The error lies in fragment II. The sentence uses the phrase 'to be subjected to' which is erroneous.

The correct phrase will be 'to subject citizens' which implies that the government is unconscionable for they have exposed the citizens to such toxic air. 

'To subject someone to something' means 'to cause or force someone to experience something harmful'.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Bhutan is a beautiful (A) / country which almost (B) / eighty percent of the (C) / population lives in villages. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part B, the relative pronoun 'which' must be replaced with 'whose', which means 'of which', as the clause is in the possessive case.

Ex 1: Q. Whose pen is this? Ans: This is Jai's pen. 
Ex 2: This is the car whose design is below standard. 

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: In each of the questions, there are 4 sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are correct in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly.
I. Indians and ‘Indian National Anthem’
II. being adjudged as the best by UNESCO
III. are probably the most common fake news
IV. going around the Internet.
questions.solution:
Fragment I: Indians and ‘Indian National Anthem’ 

In this fragment, the definite article 'the' must precede the phrase 'Indian National Anthem'.

Correct fragment: Indians and the ‘Indian National Anthem’ 

Fragment II: being adjudged as the best by UNESCO 

The verb 'adjudged' doesn't take 'as' after it so usage of it is erroenous here.

Correct fragment: being adjudged the best by UNESCO 

Fragment III: are probably the most common fake news 

The usage of the plural verb 'are' must be replaced by 'is' to make this fragment grammatically correct.

Correct fragment: is probably the most common fake news 

The fragment IV is absolutely correct.

Option A is hence the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: In each of the questions, there are 4 sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are correct in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly.
I. With our demographic spread
II. there has been an urgent need
III. to codify
IV. what is permissible social behaviour.
questions.solution:
Fragment II: there has been an urgent need 

The phrase 'an urgent need' implies that the tense has to be the present one. The correct verb will be 'is' instead of 'has been'.

Correct fragment: there is an urgent need

Fragment IV: what is permissible social behaviour. 

As it is not an interrogative sentence, the the clause will be in affirmative form.

Correct fragment: what permissible social behaviour is.

The rest of the fragments are correct.

Clearly, option B is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: The given sentence has been broken up into four different parts. The error, if any, will be in any one part of the sentence. Select the option which contains the part of the sentence which has an error (spelling, grammatical or contextual). If there is no error, choose option E.
At the age of twenty, (A) / he became Bollywood’s (B) / one of the most (C) / sought actors. (D) / No Error (E)
questions.solution:
The fragment D of the sentence is erroneous.

Reason:

The preposition ‘after’ is missing after the word ‘sought’. The preposition alters the meaning of ‘sought’ quite a bit. The preposition ‘after’ is necessary after ‘sought’ in the given context. “Sought-after” functions as an adjective.

“Seek” means ‘attempt to find (something)’. “Sought” is the past form of “seek”.

“Sought after” means ‘wanted by many people and usually of high quality or rare’.

Correct Sentence:

At the age of twenty, he became Bollywood’s one of the most sought-after actors. 

Hence, option D is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: In each of the questions, there are 4 sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are correct in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly.
I. Along with recapitalisation of public sector banks,
II. the Central Government announced a huge roads project, which will help scale up 
III. public spending of infrastructure and 
IV. boosts job creation and growth.
questions.solution:
Fragment III: public spending of infrastructure and

‘On’ will be a better preposition here in place of ‘of’ after the word ‘spending’.

Correct fragment: public spending on infrastructure and

Fragment IV: boosts job creation and growth.

As we can see that the tense used here is the future tense (refer to fragment II), the verb ‘boosts’ has to be in the preset form to agree with the tense.

Correct fragment: boost job creation and growth.

The other two fragments are correct and thus have no error in them.

Option A is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: In each of the questions, there are 4 sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are correct in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly.
I. The terrorist attack in New York confirms fears that 
II. terrorism, especially in West, is becoming 
III. more decentralised, with individuals radicalised by 
IV. terrorist ideology taking up arm on their own.
questions.solution:
Fragment II: terrorism, especially in West, is becoming

The definite article ‘the’ must precede the word ‘West’ as in the context the West refers to various nations, depending on the context, most often including at least part of Europe.

Correct fragment: terrorism, especially in the West, is becoming

Fragment IV: terrorist ideology taking up arm on their own.

The correct idiom is ‘take up arms’ and not ‘take up arm’.

Correct fragment: terrorist ideology taking up arms on their own.

The rest of the two fragments are absolutely correct.

Option C is hence the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: In each of the questions, there are 4 sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are correct in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly.
I. Just right before starting the tour, the US Secretary 
II. gave a speech on India-US ties that was 
III. so broad as it was deep, talking of the road ahead 
IV. together for the next 100 years.
questions.solution:
Fragment I: Just right before starting the tour, the US Secretary

Usage of both ‘just’ and ‘right’ brings redundancy in the sentence as both mean the same.

Correct fragment: Just before starting the tour, the US Secretary

Fragment III: so broad as it was deep, talking of the road ahead

As the sentence is in affirmative, ‘as … as’ must be used for comparison of positive degree.

Correct fragment: as broad as it was deep, talking of the road ahead

No other fragment has an error.

Evidently, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
On account of the (A) / high land prices we (B) / are setup the factory (C) / on the outskirts of the city. (D) / No error (E)
options: on the outskirts of the city
questions.solution:
In part C, use of the auxiliary verb 'are' is erroneous and hence must be replaced with 'will' to make the sentence meaningful.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: In each of the questions, there are 4 sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are correct in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly.
I. The Supreme Court’s direction that within three months 
II. there should be at least two special deposition centres for every high court’s jurisdiction
III. is a positive step towards to ensure
IV. a conducive and protective atmosphere for vulnerable witnesses.
questions.solution:
Fragment II: there should be at least two special deposition centres for every high court’s jurisdiction

Instead of ‘for’, ‘under’ would be a better preposition here in the context.

Correct fragment: there should be at least two special deposition centres under every high court’s jurisdiction

Fragment III: is a positive step towards to ensure

A gerund must follow after the preposition ‘towards’.

Correct fragment: is a positive step towards ensuring

The other two fragments are absolutely correct.

Clearly, option A is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
 You have failed to produce (A) / no evidence to support (B) / the charge that he (C) / is responsible for the error. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In the predicate part of this sentence, use of two negatives - 'failed' and 'no evidence' together is redundant and meaningless. In part B, 'no', therefore, must be replaced with 'any' to make it a grammatically correct sentence.

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Implementing these measures (A) / will help the government (B) / to save more of (C) / 400 crores each year. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part C, the phrase 'more of' must be replaced with 'more than', which means 'over', to make it a grammatically correct sentence.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
As per the agreement (A) / scientists from both of (B) / the countries will work (C) / together to find the solution. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part B, 'both' is being used as an adjective and hence the preposition 'of' which is followed by it is not required to make the sentence complete and thus can be removed.

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: The given sentence has been broken up into four different parts. The error, if any, will be in any one part of the sentence. Select the option which contains the part of the sentence which has an error (spelling, grammatical or contextual). If there is no error, choose option E.
My uncle said that (A) / he would reach to (B) / the bus stand with all his (C) / belongings within an hour. (D) / No Error (E)
options: the bus stand with all his
questions.solution:
The fragment B of the sentence is erroneous.

Reason:

The verb reach does not take a preposition before its object. Hence, the usage of the preposition “to” is erroneous after “reach” in fragment B of the sentence.

Correct Sentence:

My uncle said that he would reach the bus stand with all his belongings within an hour.

Hence, option B is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: The given sentence has been broken up into four different parts. The error, if any, will be in any one part of the sentence. Select the option which contains the part of the sentence which has an error (spelling, grammatical or contextual). If there is no error, choose option E.
Properties of the willful defaulters (A) / were confisceted by the government (B) / last month after the Enforcement Directorate (C) / filed charge sheet against them. (D) / No Error (E)
options: last month after the Enforcement Directorate
questions.solution:
The fragment B of the sentence is erroneous.

Reason:

The word “confisceted” has been misspelled in the given sentence. The correct spelling of the word is “confiscated”.

Correct Sentence:

Properties of the willful defaulters were confiscated by the government last month after the Enforcement Directorate filed charge sheet against them.

Hence, option B is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In each of the questions, there are four sentences or parts of sentences that form a paragraph. Identify the sentence/s or part/s that is/are incorrect in terms of grammar and usage and choose the answer accordingly:
I. Both revenue and profits, when compared to 
II. the results reported for the first quarter have shown 
III. signs of improvement that suggests
IV. a slow but steady pick-up in demand in the wider economy.
questions.solution:
Fragment III: signs of improvement that suggests

In this fragment, the singular case of the verb 'suggest' is erroneous. Instead of 'suggests' it should be 'suggest' to make it grammatically correct.

Correct fragment: signs of improvement that suggest

The other fragments are absolutely correct and has no error in it.

Option C is hence the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
The company, which has (A) / won the government contract, (B) / also plans to invest (C) / with the power sector. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
The vreb 'invest' is generally followed by the preposition 'in' and not 'with'. The correct formation, hence, will be- The company, which has won the government contract, also plans to invest in the power sector.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
In the circumstance (A) / the manager has (B) / no option (C) / except to resign. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
The preposition 'except' is generally followed by a gerund while 'but' is followed by an infinitive.
 
In option D, the preposition 'except', therefore, must be replaced with 'but' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
After her retiring (A) / she established many (B) / institutions to train (C) / underpriviliged but talented children. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In option A, 'retiring' must be replaced with 'retirement' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: The given sentence has been broken up into four different parts. The error, if any, will be in any one part of the sentence. Select the option which contains the part of the sentence which has an error (spelling, grammatical or contextual). If there is no error, choose option E.
The dictator sentenced the social activist (A) / to ten years of rigorous imprisonment (B) / just because she vauched for (C) / women empowerment in a blog. (D) / No Error
options: The dictator sentenced the social activist
to ten years of rigorous imprisonment
questions.solution:
The fragment C of the sentence is erroneous.

Reason:

The word “vauched” has been misspelled in the given sentence. The correct spelling of the word is “vouched”.

Correct Sentence:

The dictator sentenced the social activist to ten years of rigorous imprisonment just because she vouched for women empowerment in a blog.

Hence, option C is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
It is not worthy (A) / the company's reputation (B) / to compromise on quality (C) / for the sake of profit (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In option A, 'worthy' must be replaced with 'worth' to make the sentence grammatically correct. 
If something is worth a particular action, or if an action is worth doing, it is considered to be important enough for that action.

Ex. I am spending a lot of money and time on this boat, but it is worth it.
Ex. This restaurant is well worth a visit.
Ex. It is worth pausing to consider these statements from Mr Davies.

Hence, option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
On account of (A) / the new investment rules (B) / more companies are planning (C) / for the expansion of its business in China. (D) / No error (E)
options: for the expansion of its business in China
questions.solution:
In part D, possessive pronoun 'its' will not be used when used in context of many countries and must be replaced with 'their'.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Negotiations with the bank (A) / are at an advanced (B) / stage and we hope to (C) / signed the deal soon. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part D, as the phrase following the main verb 'hope' has to be an infinitive (to + base form of a verb), 'signed' must be replaced with 'sign' to make it a grammatically correct sentence.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
`Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
He deserves a lot (A) / of praise for all (B) / the records he achieves (C) / during his career. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part C, 'achieves' must be replaced with 'achieved' to make the sentence grammatically correct and appropriate in the context.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
 The company has launched (A) / a creative marketing campaign (B) / to reach for (C) / its rural customers. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In option C, 'for' is not required as 'reach' need not be followed by a preposition.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
 The judge has directed to (A) / the accused and the complainant (B) / not to interfere with (C) / the witness in any manner. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In option A, 'to' is not required after 'directed' to make the sentence meaningful.

Hence, option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Ram will be suspended (A) / because he opened an account (B) / without obtaining none (C) / of the necessary documents. (D) / No error
questions.solution:
In part C, two negatives 'without' and 'none' can't go together. 'None' hence must be replaced with 'any' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
To improve its efficiency (A) / all income tax officers (B) / will be provide with (C) / laptop next year. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part C, 'provide' must be replaced with 'provided' as the sentence is in passive voice and past participle of the verb is required for the sentence to be grammatically correct.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
 He was not a (A) / good manager because (B) / he did not know (C) / what to react in a crisis. (D) / No error (E)
options: what to react in a crisis
questions.solution:
In option D, 'what' must be replaced by 'how' for the sentence to make sense.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
The company recently opened (A) / its first branch in Bangalore (B) / and will expand its operations (C) / to other state sooner. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In part D, 'state' must be replaced with 'states' as under the context, it's more appropriate to use 'other states' rather than 'other state'. 

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Since the tenant failed (A) / to pay his rent on time (B) / the landlord denied him (C) / access to the premise. (D) / No error (E)
options: To pay his rent on time
questions.solution:
In option D, 'premises' must be used and not 'premise' because premises stands for a building while premise stands for an idea.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
According to the survey (A) / more of forty per cent (B) / of Indians do not (C) / have access to banks. (D) / No error  (E)
questions.solution:
Under the context, the phrase 'more of' must be replaced with the prepositional phrase 'more than', which means 'over', to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Thr Reserve Bank of India are (A) / responsible for ensuring (B) / that the banking sector (C) / functions effectively. (D) / No error (E)
options: The reserve Bank of India are
questions.solution:
As Reserve Bank of India is a single entity, therefore, 'is' should be used in place of 'are'.

Hence, option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Today most employees complain (A) / of suffering for the stress (B) / of attending to (C) / rude customers all day. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In the context of a disease, use of 'suffer from' is more appropriate than that of 'suffer for'. 

In part B, 'suffering for', hence, must be replaced with 'suffering from' to make it a grammatically correct sentence. 

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
The minister has (A) / recently announced (B) / a detail insurance plan to (C) / meet the needs of the poor. (D) / No error (E)
options: meet the needs of the poor
questions.solution:
In option C, the noun 'detail' must be replaced with the adjective 'detailed' as it is adding information to the noun phrase 'insurance plan'. 

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
 There is a shortage (A) / for qualified staff (B) / in many software (C) / companies in India. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
The noun 'shortage' is generally followed by the preposition 'of' and not 'for'. The correct formation will be- There is a shortage of qualified staff in many software companies in India. 

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'.
Mr Bajaj has resigned (A) / from the post of director (B) / which he holds (C) / for over ten years. (D) / No error (E)
questions.solution:
In option C, 'holds' must be replaced with 'held' to match the tense of the principal clause.

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
